    31→class CircError(Exception):
    32→    def __init__(self, error_type: str, message: str, file: str = None, line: int = None, col: int = None):
    33→        super().__init__(message)
    34→        self.error_type = error_type

    37→        self.line = line
    38→        self.col = col

    44→class EvalError(Exception):
    45→    def __init__(self, error_type: str, message: str):
    46→        super().__init__(message)
    47→        self.error_type = error_type
    48→        self.message = message
    49→
    50→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:

    53→
    54→@dataclass
    55→class Signal:
    56→    name: str
    57→    msb: int = 0
    58→    lsb: int = 0
    59→
    60→    @property
    61→    def width(self) -> int:
    62→        return self.msb - self.lsb + 1
    63→
    64→
    65→# AST Expression types
    66→@dataclass
    67→class Identifier:
    68→    name: str
    69→
    70→
    71→@dataclass
    72→class Literal:
    73→    value: int
    74→    width: int
    75→
    76→
    77→@dataclass
    78→class Index:
    79→    """Single bit extraction: expr[index]"""
    80→    expr: 'Expr'
    81→    index: int
    82→
    83→
    84→@dataclass
    85→class Slice:
    86→    """Bit slice: expr[hi:lo]"""
    87→    expr: 'Expr'
    88→    hi: int
    89→    lo: int
    90→
    91→
    92→@dataclass
    93→class Concat:
    94→    """Concatenation: {e1, e2, ..., ek} - e1 is MSB"""
    95→    parts: list
    96→
    97→
    98→@dataclass
    99→class Call:
   100→    operator: str
   101→    args: list
   102→
   103→
   104→Expr = (Identifier, Literal, Index, Slice, Concat, Call)
   105→
   106→# (min_args, max_args) where None means unlimited
   107→OPERATOR_ARITY = {
   108→    'NOT': (1, 1), 'BUF': (1, 1),
   109→    'AND': (2, None), 'OR': (2, None), 'XOR': (2, None),
   110→    'NAND': (2, None), 'NOR': (2, None), 'XNOR': (2, None),
   111→    'MUX': (3, 3), 'ITE': (3, 3),
   112→    'REDUCE_AND': (1, 1), 'REDUCE_OR': (1, 1), 'REDUCE_XOR': (1, 1),
   113→    'EQ': (2, 2),
   114→}
   115→
   116→# Category sets for width/eval dispatch
   117→_UNARY_PROPAGATE = frozenset({'NOT', 'BUF'})
   118→_BINARY_PROPAGATE = frozenset({'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'})
   119→_SELECT = frozenset({'MUX', 'ITE'})
   120→_REDUCE = frozenset({'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR'})
   121→
   122→
   123→def _expr_width(expr, signal_widths: dict) -> int:
   124→    """Compute the bit-width of an expression. signal_widths maps signal names to widths."""
   125→    if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   126→        return expr.width
   127→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   128→        return signal_widths[expr.name]
   129→    if isinstance(expr, Index):
   130→        return 1
   131→    if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   132→        return expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   133→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   134→        return sum(_expr_width(p, signal_widths) for p in expr.parts)
   135→    if isinstance(expr, Call):
   136→        return _call_result_width(expr, signal_widths)
   137→    raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   138→
   139→
   140→def _call_result_width(call: Call, signal_widths: dict) -> int:
   141→    op = call.operator
   142→    if op in _UNARY_PROPAGATE | _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
   143→        return _expr_width(call.args[0], signal_widths)
   144→    if op in _SELECT:
   145→        return _expr_width(call.args[1], signal_widths)
   146→    if op in _REDUCE | {'EQ'}:
   147→        return 1
   148→    raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   149→
   150→
   151→def _collect_dependencies(expr) -> set:
   152→    """Collect all signal names referenced in an expression."""
   153→    if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   154→        return {expr.name}
   155→    if isinstance(expr, (Index, Slice)):
   156→        return _collect_dependencies(expr.expr)
   157→    if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   158→        deps = set()
   159→        for part in expr.parts:
   160→            deps.update(_collect_dependencies(part))
   161→        return deps
   162→    if isinstance(expr, Call):
   163→        deps = set()
   164→        for arg in expr.args:
   165→            deps.update(_collect_dependencies(arg))
   166→        return deps
   167→    return set()
   168→
   169→
   170→def parse_literal(text: str, filename: str = None, line: int = None, col: int = None) -> Tuple[int, int]:
   171→    """Parse a literal value. Returns (value, width)."""
   172→    original = text
   173→    text = text.replace('_', '')
   174→
   175→    if text == 'X':
   176→        raise CircError("CircParseError", "Literal 'X' values are not allowed in .circ files", filename, line, col)
   177→
   178→    if text in ('0', '1'):
   179→        return int(text), 1
   180→
   181→    if text.lower().startswith('0b'):
   182→        digits = text[2:]
   183→        if not digits:
   184→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid binary literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   185→        if not re.match(r'^[01]+$', digits):
   186→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid binary literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   187→        return int(digits, 2), len(digits)
   188→
   189→    if text.lower().startswith('0x'):
   190→        digits = text[2:]
   191→        if not digits:
   192→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid hex literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   193→        if not re.match(r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', digits):
   194→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid hex literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   195→        value = int(digits, 16)
   196→        width = len(digits) * 4
   197→        return value, width
   198→
   199→    sized_match = re.match(r"^(\d+)'([bhd])([0-9a-fA-F_]+)$", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   200→    if sized_match:
   201→        width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   202→        base = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   203→        digits = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   204→
   205→        if width <= 0:
   206→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal width: {original}", filename, line, col)
   207→
   208→        base_validators = {
   209→            'b': (r'^[01]+$', 2, f"Invalid binary literal: {original}"),
   210→            'h': (r'^[0-9a-fA-F]+$', 16, f"Invalid hex literal: {original}"),
   211→            'd': (r'^[0-9]+$', 10, f"Invalid decimal literal: {original}"),
   212→        }
   213→        pattern, base_int, err_msg = base_validators[base]
   214→        if not re.match(pattern, digits):
   215→            raise CircError("CircParseError", err_msg, filename, line, col)
   216→        try:
   217→            value = int(digits, base_int)
   218→        except ValueError:
   219→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   220→
   221→        max_val = (1 << width) - 1
   222→        if value > max_val:
   223→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Sized decimal value {value} exceeds width {width}", filename, line, col)
   224→        return value, width
   225→
   226→    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid literal: {original}", filename, line, col)
   227→
   228→
   229→def format_value(value: int, width: int, radix: str) -> str:
   230→    if width == 1:
   231→        return str(value & 1)
   232→    if radix == 'bin':
   233→        return f"0b{bin(value)[2:].zfill(width)}"
   234→    if radix == 'hex':
   235→        hex_width = (width + 3) // 4
   236→        return f"0x{hex(value)[2:].zfill(hex_width)}"
   237→    return str(value)
   238→
   239→
   240→def _split_comma_separated(text: str) -> list:
   241→    """Split text by commas respecting brace and paren nesting. Returns list of stripped parts."""
   242→    parts = []
   243→    current = ""
   244→    brace_depth = 0
   245→    paren_depth = 0
   246→    for ch in text:
   247→        if ch == '{':
   248→            brace_depth += 1
   249→            current += ch
   250→        elif ch == '}':
   251→            brace_depth -= 1
   252→            current += ch
   253→        elif ch == '(':
   254→            paren_depth += 1
   255→            current += ch
   256→        elif ch == ')':
   257→            paren_depth -= 1
   258→            current += ch
   259→        elif ch == ',' and brace_depth == 0 and paren_depth == 0:
   260→            parts.append(current.strip())
   261→            current = ""
   262→        else:
   263→            current += ch
   264→    if current.strip():
   265→        parts.append(current.strip())
   266→    return parts
   267→
   268→
   269→class Parser:
   270→    def __init__(self, content: str, filename: str):
   271→        self.filename = filename
   272→        self.lines = content.split('\n')
   273→
   274→    def parse(self) -> 'Circuit':
   275→        circuit = Circuit()
   276→        in_declarations = True
   277→        for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(self.lines, 1):
   278→            stripped = raw_line.strip()
   279→            if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   280→                continue
   281→            if self._is_declaration(stripped):
   282→                if not in_declarations:
   283→                    raise CircError("DeclarationAfterAssignmentError", "Declaration after assignment is not allowed",
   284→                                   self.filename, line_num, 1)
   285→                self._parse_declaration(stripped, circuit, line_num)
   286→            elif '=' in stripped:
   287→                in_declarations = False
   288→                self._parse_assignment(stripped, circuit, line_num, raw_line)
   289→            else:
   290→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {stripped}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   291→        return circuit
   292→
   293→    def _is_declaration(self, line: str) -> bool:
   294→        parts = line.split()
   295→        return '=' not in line and bool(parts) and parts[0].upper() in ('INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE')
   296→
   297→    def _parse_declaration(self, line: str, circuit: 'Circuit', line_num: int):
   298→        parts = line.split()
   299→        if not parts:
   300→            return
   301→        keyword = parts[0].upper()
   302→        target_map = {'INPUT': circuit.inputs, 'OUTPUT': circuit.outputs, 'WIRE': circuit.wires}
   303→        if keyword not in target_map:
   304→            return
   305→        target = target_map[keyword]
   306→        existing_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   307→        for part in parts[1:]:
   308→            vec_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\[(\d+):(\d+)\]$', part)
   309→            if vec_match:
   310→                name, msb, lsb = vec_match.group(1), int(vec_match.group(2)), int(vec_match.group(3))
   311→                if msb < lsb:
   312→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: msb ({msb}) must be >= lsb ({lsb})",
   313→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   314→                if lsb < 0:
   315→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: lsb ({lsb}) must be >= 0",
   316→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   317→                if name in existing_names:
   318→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}",
   319→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   320→                target.append(Signal(name, msb, lsb))
   321→                existing_names.add(name)
   322→            else:
   323→                if not self._is_valid_identifier(part):
   324→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid identifier: {part}",
   325→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   326→                if part in existing_names:
   327→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {part}",
   328→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   329→                target.append(Signal(part, 0, 0))
   330→                existing_names.add(part)
   331→
   332→    @staticmethod
   333→    def _is_valid_identifier(name: str) -> bool:
   334→        return bool(name) and not name[0].isdigit() and all(c.isalnum() or c == '_' for c in name)
   335→
   336→    def _parse_assignment(self, line: str, circuit: 'Circuit', line_num: int, raw_line: str):
   337→        eq_pos = line.find('=')
   338→        lhs = line[:eq_pos].strip()
   339→        rhs = line[eq_pos + 1:].strip()
   340→        if not lhs:
   341→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Missing left-hand side in assignment", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   342→        if not re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)$', lhs):
   343→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid left-hand side: {lhs}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   344→        rhs_expr, _ = self._parse_expression(rhs, line_num, eq_pos + 2)
   345→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, raw_line.find(lhs) + 1))
   346→
   347→    def _parse_expression(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   348→        text = text.strip()
   349→        if not text:
   350→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   351→        if text.startswith('{'):
   352→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   353→        expr, consumed = self._parse_primary_expr(text, line_num, start_col)
   354→        remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   355→        while remaining.startswith('['):
   356→            bracket_end = remaining.find(']')
   357→            if bracket_end == -1:
   358→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched bracket in index/slice", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   359→            bracket_content = remaining[1:bracket_end]
   360→            if ':' in bracket_content:
   361→                parts = bracket_content.split(':')
   362→                if len(parts) != 2:
   363→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   364→                try:
   365→                    hi, lo = int(parts[0].strip()), int(parts[1].strip())
   366→                except ValueError:
   367→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice indices: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   368→                expr = Slice(expr, hi, lo)
   369→            else:
   370→                try:
   371→                    idx = int(bracket_content.strip())
   372→                except ValueError:
   373→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid index: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   374→                expr = Index(expr, idx)
   375→            consumed += bracket_end + 1
   376→            remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   377→        return expr, consumed
   378→
   379→    def _try_match_literal(self, text: str):
   380→        m = re.match(r"^(\d+'[bhd])", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   381→        if m:
   382→            prefix = m.group(1).lower()
   383→            rest = text[len(m.group(1)):]
   384→            if prefix.endswith('b'):
   385→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([01_]+)', rest)
   386→            elif prefix.endswith('h'):
   387→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   388→            else:
   389→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9_]+)', rest)
   390→            if digits_m:
   391→                full = m.group(1) + digits_m.group(1)
   392→                end_pos = len(full)
   393→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   394→                    return full
   395→            return None
   396→        if text.lower().startswith('0b') and len(text) > 2:
   397→            m = re.match(r'^(0b[01_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   398→            if m:
   399→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   400→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   401→                    return m.group(1)
   402→            return None
   403→        if text.lower().startswith('0x') and len(text) > 2:
   404→            m = re.match(r'^(0x[0-9a-fA-F_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   405→            if m:
   406→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   407→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   408→                    return m.group(1)
   409→            return None
   410→        if text and text[0] in ('0', '1'):
   411→            if len(text) == 1 or not text[1].isalnum() and text[1] != '_':
   412→                return text[0]
   413→        return None
   414→
   415→    def _parse_primary_expr(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   416→        text = text.strip()
   417→        if not text:
   418→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   419→        if text.startswith('{'):
   420→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   421→        if text.startswith('('):
   422→            depth, i = 1, 1
   423→            while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   424→                if text[i] == '(':
   425→                    depth += 1
   426→                elif text[i] == ')':
   427→                    depth -= 1
   428→                i += 1
   429→            if depth != 0:
   430→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   431→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(text[1:i-1], line_num, start_col + 1)
   432→            return expr, i
   433→        lit_text = self._try_match_literal(text)
   434→        if lit_text is not None:
   435→            value, width = parse_literal(lit_text, self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   436→            return Literal(value, width), len(lit_text)
   437→        call_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\(', text)
   438→        if call_match:
   439→            op_name = call_match.group(1).upper()
   440→            op_start = call_match.end()
   441→            paren_count, i = 1, op_start
   442→            while i < len(text) and paren_count > 0:
   443→                if text[i] == '(':
   444→                    paren_count += 1
   445→                elif text[i] == ')':
   446→                    paren_count -= 1
   447→                i += 1
   448→            if paren_count != 0:
   449→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis in expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   450→            args = self._parse_arguments(text[op_start:i-1], line_num, start_col + op_start)
   451→            return Call(op_name, args), i
   452→        id_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)', text)
   453→        if id_match:
   454→            name = id_match.group(1)
   455→            return Identifier(name), len(name)
   456→        raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid expression: {text[:50]}", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   457→
   458→    def _parse_concatenation(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[Concat, int]:
   459→        if not text.startswith('{'):
   460→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Expected '{' for concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   461→        depth, i = 1, 1
   462→        while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   463→            if text[i] == '{':
   464→                depth += 1
   465→            elif text[i] == '}':
   466→                depth -= 1
   467→            i += 1
   468→        if depth != 0:
   469→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched brace in concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   470→        inner = text[1:i-1]
   471→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(inner)
   472→        if not raw_parts:
   473→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   474→        parts = [self._parse_expression(p, line_num, start_col + 1 + idx)[0] for idx, p in enumerate(raw_parts)]
   475→        return Concat(parts), i
   476→
   477→    def _parse_arguments(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> list:
   478→        text = text.strip()
   479→        if not text:
   480→            return []
   481→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(text)
   482→        args = []
   483→        offset = 0
   484→        for raw in raw_parts:
   485→            idx = text.index(raw, offset)
   486→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(raw, line_num, start_col + idx)
   487→            args.append(expr)
   488→            offset = idx + len(raw)
   489→        return args
   490→

   500→@dataclass
   501→class Circuit:
   502→    inputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   503→    outputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   504→    wires: list = field(default_factory=list)
   505→    assignments: list = field(default_factory=list)
   506→
   507→    def get_all_names(self) -> set:
   508→        return {sig.name for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires}
   509→
   510→    def get_signal(self, name: str):
   511→        for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires:
   512→            if sig.name == name:
   513→                return sig
   514→        return None

   517→class Validator:
   518→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit, filename: str):
   519→        self.circuit = circuit
   520→        self.filename = filename
   521→        self.signal_types = {sig.name: 'input' for sig in circuit.inputs}
   522→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'output' for sig in circuit.outputs})
   523→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'wire' for sig in circuit.wires})
   524→        self.signal_info = {sig.name: sig for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
   525→        self._all_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   526→        self._signal_widths = {name: sig.width for name, sig in self.signal_info.items()}
   527→
   528→    def validate(self):
   529→        self._check_assignments()
   530→        self._check_cycles()
   531→
   532→    def _check_assignments(self):
   533→        assigned = set()
   534→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   535→            if asn.lhs not in self._all_names:
   536→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   537→            if self.signal_types.get(asn.lhs) == 'input':
   538→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   539→            if asn.lhs in assigned:
   540→                raise CircError("MultipleAssignmentError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   541→            assigned.add(asn.lhs)
   542→            self._validate_expr(asn.rhs, asn.line)
   543→        for sig in self.circuit.outputs + self.circuit.wires:
   544→            if sig.name not in assigned:
   545→                kind = "Output" if self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output' else "Wire"
   546→                raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", self.filename)
   547→
   548→    def _validate_expr(self, expr, line_num: int) -> int:
   549→        """Validate expression and return its width."""
   550→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   551→            return expr.width
   552→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   553→            if expr.name not in self._all_names:
   554→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", self.filename, line_num)
   555→            return self._signal_widths[expr.name]
   556→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
   557→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   558→            self._check_index_bounds(expr, line_num)
   559→            return 1
   560→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   561→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   562→            if expr.hi < expr.lo:
   563→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: hi ({expr.hi}) < lo ({expr.lo})",
   564→                               self.filename, line_num)
   565→            self._check_slice_bounds(expr, line_num)
   566→            return expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   567→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   568→            return sum(self._validate_expr(p, line_num) for p in expr.parts)
   569→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   570→            self._check_operator_arity(expr, line_num)
   571→            return self._check_operator_width(expr, line_num)
   572→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   573→
   574→    def _check_index_bounds(self, expr: Index, line_num: int):
   575→        sig = self.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
   576→        if sig:
   577→            if expr.index < sig.lsb or expr.index > sig.msb:
   578→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   579→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
   580→                               self.filename, line_num)
   581→        else:
   582→            base_width = self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   583→            if expr.index < 0 or expr.index >= base_width:
   584→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   585→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
   586→                               self.filename, line_num)
   587→
   588→    def _check_slice_bounds(self, expr: Slice, line_num: int):
   589→        sig = self.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
   590→        if sig:
   591→            if expr.lo < sig.lsb or expr.hi > sig.msb:
   592→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   593→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
   594→                               self.filename, line_num)
   595→        else:
   596→            base_width = self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   597→            if expr.lo < 0 or expr.hi >= base_width:
   598→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   599→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
   600→                               self.filename, line_num)
   601→
   602→    def _check_operator_arity(self, call: Call, line_num: int):
   603→        op = call.operator
   604→        num_args = len(call.args)
   605→        if op not in OPERATOR_ARITY:
   606→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Unknown operator: {op}", self.filename, line_num)
   607→        min_args, max_args = OPERATOR_ARITY[op]
   608→        if num_args < min_args or (max_args and num_args > max_args):
   609→            if max_args == min_args:
   610→                req = f"exactly {min_args}"
   611→            elif max_args is None:
   612→                req = f"at least {min_args}"
   613→            else:
   614→                req = f"between {min_args} and {max_args}"
   615→            plural = "s" if min_args > 1 or (max_args and max_args > 1) else ""
   616→            raise CircError("ArityError", f"Operator {op} requires {req} argument{plural}, got {num_args}", self.filename, line_num)
   617→
   618→    def _check_operator_width(self, call: Call, line_num: int) -> int:
   619→        op = call.operator
   620→        arg_widths = [self._validate_expr(arg, line_num) for arg in call.args]
   621→        if op in _UNARY_PROPAGATE:
   622→            return arg_widths[0]
   623→        if op in _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
   624→            for i, w in enumerate(arg_widths[1:], 1):
   625→                if w != arg_widths[0]:
   626→                    raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   627→                                   f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand {i} has width {w}",
   628→                                   self.filename, line_num)
   629→            return arg_widths[0]
   630→        if op in _SELECT:
   631→            if arg_widths[0] != 1:
   632→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   633→                               f"Selector in {op} must have width 1, got width {arg_widths[0]}", self.filename, line_num)
   634→            if arg_widths[1] != arg_widths[2]:
   635→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   636→                               f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}, operand 2 has width {arg_widths[2]}",
   637→                               self.filename, line_num)
   638→            return arg_widths[1]
   639→        if op in _REDUCE:
   640→            return 1
   641→        if op == 'EQ':
   642→            if arg_widths[0] != arg_widths[1]:
   643→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   644→                               f"Width mismatch in EQ: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}",
   645→                               self.filename, line_num)
   646→            return 1
   647→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   648→
   649→    def _check_cycles(self):
   650→        dependencies = {sig.name: set() for sig in self.circuit.inputs}
   651→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   652→            dependencies[asn.lhs] = _collect_dependencies(asn.rhs)
   653→        WHITE, GRAY, BLACK = 0, 1, 2
   654→        color = {name: WHITE for name in self._all_names}
   655→
   656→        def dfs(node: str, path: list):
   657→            color[node] = GRAY
   658→            path.append(node)
   659→            for dep in dependencies.get(node, set()):
   660→                if color[dep] == GRAY:
   661→                    cycle_start = path.index(dep)
   662→                    return path[cycle_start:] + [dep]
   663→                if color[dep] == WHITE:
   664→                    result = dfs(dep, path)
   665→                    if result:
   666→                        return result
   667→            path.pop()
   668→            color[node] = BLACK
   669→            return None
   670→
   671→        for node in self._all_names:
   672→            if color[node] == WHITE:
   673→                cycle = dfs(node, [])
   674→                if cycle:
   675→                    raise CircError("CycleError", f"Cycle detected: {' -> '.join(cycle)}", self.filename)
   676→

   678→class Evaluator:
   679→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit):
   680→        self.circuit = circuit
   681→        self.values = {}
   682→        self._signal_widths = {sig.name: sig.width for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
   683→
   684→    def evaluate(self, inputs: dict) -> dict:
   685→        self.values = dict(inputs)
   686→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   687→            self.values[asn.lhs] = self._eval_expr(asn.rhs)
   688→        return {sig.name: self.values[sig.name] for sig in self.circuit.outputs}
   689→
   690→    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> int:
   691→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   692→            return expr.value
   693→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   694→            return self.values[expr.name]
   695→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
   696→            return (self._eval_expr(expr.expr) >> expr.index) & 1
   697→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   698→            width = expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   699→            return (self._eval_expr(expr.expr) >> expr.lo) & ((1 << width) - 1)
   700→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   701→            result = 0
   702→            for part in expr.parts:
   703→                width = _expr_width(part, self._signal_widths)
   704→                result = (result << width) | (self._eval_expr(part) & ((1 << width) - 1))
   705→            return result
   706→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   707→            args = [self._eval_expr(arg) for arg in expr.args]
   708→            return self._apply_operator(expr.operator, args, expr)
   709→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   710→
   711→    def _apply_operator(self, op: str, args: list, call: Call) -> int:
   712→        if op == 'NOT':
   713→            width = _expr_width(call.args[0], self._signal_widths)
   714→            return (~args[0]) & ((1 << width) - 1)
   715→        if op == 'BUF':
   716→            return args[0]
   717→        if op in _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
   718→            width = _expr_width(call.args[0], self._signal_widths)
   719→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
   720→            result = args[0]
   721→            for a in args[1:]:
   722→                if op in ('AND', 'NAND'):
   723→                    result &= a
   724→                elif op in ('OR', 'NOR'):
   725→                    result |= a
   726→                else:
   727→                    result ^= a
   728→            if op in ('NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
   729→                result = (~result) & mask
   730→            return result
   731→        if op in _SELECT:
   732→            return args[1] if args[0] else args[2]
   733→        if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
   734→            width = _expr_width(call.args[0], self._signal_widths)
   735→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
   736→            return 1 if (args[0] & mask) == mask else 0
   737→        if op == 'REDUCE_OR':
   738→            return 1 if args[0] != 0 else 0
   739→        if op == 'REDUCE_XOR':
   740→            val = args[0]
   741→            result = 0
   742→            while val:
   743→                result ^= (val & 1)
   744→                val >>= 1
   745→            return result
   746→        if op == 'EQ':
   747→            return 1 if args[0] == args[1] else 0
   748→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   749→

   755→def load_circuit(filename: str, command: str, json_mode: bool):
   756→    try:
   757→        with open(filename, 'r') as f:
   758→            content = f.read()
   759→    except (FileNotFoundError, IOError):
   760→        return output_error(CircError("FileNotFoundError", f"Cannot read file: {filename}", filename), command, json_mode)
   761→    try:
   762→        circuit = Parser(content, filename).parse()
   763→        Validator(circuit, filename).validate()
   764→    except CircError as e:
   765→        return output_error(e, command, json_mode)
   766→    return circuit

   769→def signal_list(signals):
   770→    return [{"name": s.name, "msb": s.msb, "lsb": s.lsb} for s in sorted(signals, key=lambda s: s.name)]
   771→
   772→
   773→def get_exit_code(error_type: str) -> int:
   774→    if error_type in VALIDATION_ERRORS:
   775→        return EXIT_VALIDATION_ERROR
   776→    return ERROR_EXIT_CODES.get(error_type, 1)
   777→
   778→
   779→def output_error(error, command: str, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   780→    exit_code = get_exit_code(error.error_type)
   781→    if json_mode:
   782→        output_json({"ok": False, "command": command, "exit_code": exit_code, "error": error.to_dict()})
   783→    else:
   784→        loc = ""
   785→        if hasattr(error, 'file') and error.file:
   786→            loc = f"{error.file}:"
   787→            if error.line:
   788→                loc += f"{error.line}:"
   789→                if error.col:
   790→                    loc += f"{error.col}:"
   791→            loc += " "
   792→        print(f"{loc}Error: {error.message}", file=sys.stderr)
   793→    return exit_code

   796→def check_command(args: list, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   797→    if not args:
   798→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
   799→    filename = args[0]
   800→    result = load_circuit(filename, "check", json_mode)
   801→    if isinstance(result, int):
   802→        return result
   803→    circuit = result
   804→    inputs = signal_list(circuit.inputs)
   805→    outputs = signal_list(circuit.outputs)
   806→    if json_mode:
   807→        output_json({"ok": True, "command": "check", "format": "circ", "inputs": inputs, "outputs": outputs})
   808→    else:
   809→        print("Circuit is valid.")
   810→        print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in inputs)}")
   811→        print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(s['name'] for s in outputs)}")
   812→    return 0

   815→def parse_input_value(value_str: str, expected_width: int, signal_name: str, filename: str = None) -> int:
   816→    try:
   817→        value, width = parse_literal(value_str, filename)
   818→    except CircError:
   819→        raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {value_str}")
   820→    if width != expected_width:
   821→        raise EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",
   822→                       f"Input width mismatch for '{signal_name}': expected width {expected_width}, got width {width}")
   823→    return value

   826→def eval_command(args: list, json_mode: bool) -> int:
   827→    if not args:
   828→        return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "Missing required argument: <file.circ>"), "__cli__", json_mode)
   829→    filename = args[0]
   830→    set_values = {}
   831→    default_value = None
   832→    allow_extra = False
   833→    radix = 'bin'
   834→    i = 1
   835→    while i < len(args):
   836→        arg = args[i]
   837→        if arg == '--set':
   838→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
   839→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--set requires a name=value argument"), "eval", json_mode)
   840→            set_arg = args[i + 1]
   841→            if '=' not in set_arg:
   842→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --set format: {set_arg}, expected name=value"), "eval", json_mode)
   843→            name, value = set_arg.split('=', 1)
   844→            set_values[name] = value
   845→            i += 2
   846→        elif arg == '--default':
   847→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
   848→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--default requires a value (0 or 1)"), "eval", json_mode)
   849→            default_arg = args[i + 1]
   850→            if default_arg not in ('0', '1'):
   851→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --default value: {default_arg}, must be 0 or 1"), "eval", json_mode)
   852→            default_value = int(default_arg)
   853→            i += 2
   854→        elif arg == '--allow-extra':
   855→            allow_extra = True
   856→            i += 1
   857→        elif arg == '--radix':
   858→            if i + 1 >= len(args):
   859→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", "--radix requires a value (bin, hex, or dec)"), "eval", json_mode)
   860→            radix_arg = args[i + 1].lower()
   861→            if radix_arg not in ('bin', 'hex', 'dec'):
   862→                return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Invalid --radix value: {radix_arg}, must be bin, hex, or dec"), "eval", json_mode)
   863→            radix = radix_arg
   864→            i += 2
   865→        elif '=' in arg:
   866→            name, value = arg.split('=', 1)
   867→            set_values[name] = value
   868→            i += 1
   869→        else:
   870→            return output_error(CircError("CliUsageError", f"Unknown option: {arg}"), "eval", json_mode)
   871→
   872→    result = load_circuit(filename, "eval", json_mode)
   873→    if isinstance(result, int):
   874→        return result
   875→    circuit = result
   876→    signal_info = {sig.name: sig for sig in circuit.inputs}
   877→    input_names = set(signal_info.keys())
   878→
   879→    for name in set_values:
   880→        if name not in input_names and not allow_extra:
   881→            return output_error(EvalError("UnknownInputError", f"Unknown input: {name}"), "eval", json_mode)
   882→
   883→    missing_inputs = input_names - set(set_values.keys())
   884→    if missing_inputs and default_value is None:
   885→        return output_error(EvalError("MissingInputError", f"Missing input values for: {', '.join(sorted(missing_inputs))}"), "eval", json_mode)
   886→
   887→    inputs = {}
   888→    try:
   889→        for sig in circuit.inputs:
   890→            if sig.name in set_values:
   891→                value = parse_input_value(set_values[sig.name], sig.width, sig.name, filename)
   892→                inputs[sig.name] = value
   893→            elif default_value is not None:
   894→                if sig.width == 1:
   895→                    inputs[sig.name] = default_value
   896→                else:
   897→                    inputs[sig.name] = default_value * ((1 << sig.width) - 1) if default_value else 0
   898→    except EvalError as e:
   899→        return output_error(e, "eval", json_mode)
   900→
   901→    try:
   902→        results = Evaluator(circuit).evaluate(inputs)
   903→    except Exception as e:
   904→        return output_error(CircError("InternalError", str(e)), "eval", json_mode)
   905→
   906→    sorted_outputs = sorted(results.items(), key=lambda x: x[0])
   907→    if json_mode:
   908→        output_list = []
   909→        for name, value in sorted_outputs:
   910→            sig = circuit.get_signal(name)
   911→            output_list.append({
   912→                "name": name,
   913→                "msb": sig.msb if sig else 0,
   914→                "lsb": sig.lsb if sig else 0,
   915→                "value": format_value(value, sig.width if sig else 1, radix)
   916→            })
   917→        output_json({
   918→            "ok": True,
   919→            "command": "eval",
   920→            "mode": "2val",
   921→            "radix": radix,
   922→            "inputs": [{"name": s.name, "msb": s.msb, "lsb": s.lsb} for s in sorted(circuit.inputs, key=lambda x: x.name)],
   923→            "outputs": output_list
   924→        })
   925→    else:
   926→        for name, value in sorted_outputs:
   927→            sig = circuit.get_signal(name)
   928→            width = sig.width if sig else 1
   929→            print(f"{name}={format_value(value, width, radix)}")
   930→    return 0

- **`_parse_expression(text, line_num, start_col)`** (line 347): Top-level expression parser. Handles concatenation (`{...}`), primary expressions, and postfix indexing/slicing (`[i]` or `[hi:lo]`).

- **`_try_match_literal(text)`** (line 379): Tries to match a literal at the start of `text`. Returns the matched string or None. Handles all literal formats with lookahead to avoid consuming partial identifiers.

- **`_parse_primary_expr(text, line_num, start_col)`** (line 415): Parses a primary expression: concatenation, parenthesized group, literal, function call (`OP(args...)`), or identifier.

- **`_parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)`** (line 458): Parses `{expr1, expr2, ...}`. First finds matching `}`, then splits inner by commas respecting nesting, then parses each part.

```python
class Evaluator:
    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit):
        self.circuit = circuit
        self.values = {}
        self._signal_widths = {sig.name: sig.width for sig in ...}

    def evaluate(self, inputs: dict) -> dict:
        self.values = dict(inputs)
        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
            self.values[asn.lhs] = self._eval_expr(asn.rhs)
        return {sig.name: self.values[sig.name] for sig in self.circuit.outputs}

    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> int:
        # Literal: return value
        # Identifier: return values[name]
        # Index: bit extraction via shift-and-mask
        # Slice: multi-bit extraction via shift-and-mask
        # Concat: shift-and-OR each part (e1 is MSB)
        # Call: evaluate args then apply operator

    def _apply_operator(self, op, args, call) -> int:
        # NOT: bitwise NOT masked to width
        # BUF: identity
        # AND/OR/XOR/NAND/NOR/XNOR: bitwise, multi-arg chaining, negate if NAND/NOR/XNOR
        # MUX/ITE: args[1] if args[0] else args[2]
        # REDUCE_AND: 1 if all bits set
        # REDUCE_OR: 1 if any bit set
        # REDUCE_XOR: XOR of all bits (parity)
        # EQ: 1 if equal, 0 otherwise
```

**Values are represented as Python `int`** throughout. All arithmetic is bitwise on integers. Width is tracked separately (not encoded in the value). Masking to width is done where needed (`(1 << width) - 1`).

**Step-by-step flow:**

1. **Argument parsing** (lines 827-870): Parses `<file.circ>` followed by options:

   - `--allow-extra`: Allows `--set` names that are not actual inputs (silently ignored)
   - `--radix bin|hex|dec`: Output format for values (default: `bin`)

3. **Resolve inputs** (lines 876-899):

4. **Evaluate** (lines 901-904): Calls `Evaluator(circuit).evaluate(inputs)`.

def parse_input_value(value_str: str, expected_width: int, signal_name: str, filename: str = None) -> int:
    try:
        value, width = parse_literal(value_str, filename)
    except CircError:
        raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {value_str}")
    if width != expected_width:
        raise EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",

    return value
```

The value string is parsed with `parse_literal()` (same parser used for circuit file literals). The parsed width must exactly match the signal's expected width. Any `CircError` from parsing is caught and re-raised as an `EvalError`.

    if isinstance(expr, Literal):    return expr.width
    if isinstance(expr, Identifier): return signal_widths[expr.name]

    if isinstance(expr, Call):       return _call_result_width(expr, signal_widths)

def _call_result_width(call, signal_widths):

    if op in _SELECT:                              return width of second arg (the "then" value)
    if op in _REDUCE | {'EQ'}:                     return 1

### 7. Literal Parsing (lines 170-226) -- How values are parsed

`parse_literal(text, filename, line, col)` returns `(value: int, width: int)`. Supported formats:

| Format | Example | Width |
|---|---|---|
| Single bit | `0`, `1` | 1 |
| Binary prefix | `0b1010` | number of digits |
| Hex prefix | `0xFF` | digits * 4 |
| Sized binary | `4'b1010` | explicit (4) |
| Sized hex | `8'hFF` | explicit (8) |
| Sized decimal | `4'd9` | explicit (4) |

Underscores are allowed as separators in all formats. The literal `X` is explicitly forbidden.

### 10. Parser Class (lines 269-489) -- How circuit files are parsed

The `Parser` class has these methods:

- **`parse()`** (line 274): Main entry point. Iterates over lines. First pass collects declarations (INPUT/OUTPUT/WIRE), then switches to assignment parsing once an `=` is seen. No declarations allowed after assignments.

- **`_is_declaration(line)`** (line 293): Returns True if the line has no `=` and starts with INPUT/OUTPUT/WIRE (case-insensitive keyword).

- **`_parse_declaration(line, circuit, line_num)`** (line 297): Parses signal declarations. Supports both scalar signals (`INPUT a`) and vector signals (`INPUT bus[7:0]`). Enforces: msb >= lsb, lsb >= 0, no duplicate names, valid identifiers.

- **`_parse_assignment(line, circuit, line_num, raw_line)`** (line 336): Splits on `=`, validates LHS is a valid identifier, parses RHS as an expression.

- **`_parse_expression(text, line_num, start_col)`** (line 347): Top-level expression parser. Handles concatenation (`{...}`), primary expressions, and postfix indexing/slicing (`[i]` or `[hi:lo]`).

- **`_try_match_literal(text)`** (line 379): Tries to match a literal at the start of `text`. Returns the matched string or None. Handles all literal formats with lookahead to avoid consuming partial identifiers.

- **`_parse_primary_expr(text, line_num, start_col)`** (line 415): Parses a primary expression: concatenation, parenthesized group, literal, function call (`OP(args...)`), or identifier.

- **`_parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)`** (line 458): Parses `{expr1, expr2, ...}`. First finds matching `}`, then splits inner by commas respecting nesting, then parses each part.

- **`_parse_arguments(text, line_num, start_col)`** (line 477): Parses comma-separated argument list inside parentheses.

class Evaluator:
    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit):
        self.circuit = circuit
        self.values = {}
        self._signal_widths = {sig.name: sig.width for sig in ...}

    def evaluate(self, inputs: dict) -> dict:
        self.values = dict(inputs)
        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
            self.values[asn.lhs] = self._eval_expr(asn.rhs)
        return {sig.name: self.values[sig.name] for sig in self.circuit.outputs}

    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> int:
        # Literal: return value
        # Identifier: return values[name]
        # Index: bit extraction via shift-and-mask
        # Slice: multi-bit extraction via shift-and-mask
        # Concat: shift-and-OR each part (e1 is MSB)
        # Call: evaluate args then apply operator

    def _apply_operator(self, op, args, call) -> int:
        # NOT: bitwise NOT masked to width
        # BUF: identity
        # AND/OR/XOR/NAND/NOR/XNOR: bitwise, multi-arg chaining, negate if NAND/NOR/XNOR
        # MUX/ITE: args[1] if args[0] else args[2]
        # REDUCE_AND: 1 if all bits set
        # REDUCE_OR: 1 if any bit set
        # REDUCE_XOR: XOR of all bits (parity)
        # EQ: 1 if equal, 0 otherwise
```

**Values are represented as Python `int`** throughout. All arithmetic is bitwise on integers. Width is tracked separately (not encoded in the value). Masking to width is done where needed (`(1 << width) - 1`).

### 14. eval_command Function (lines 826-930) -- How the eval command works

**Step-by-step flow:**

1. **Argument parsing** (lines 827-870): Parses `<file.circ>` followed by options:
   - `--set name=value` or bare `name=value`: Sets an input signal value
   - `--default 0|1`: Default value for unspecified inputs (expanded to all-0 or all-1 for multi-bit)
   - `--allow-extra`: Allows `--set` names that are not actual inputs (silently ignored)
   - `--radix bin|hex|dec`: Output format for values (default: `bin`)

2. **Load and validate circuit** (line 872): Calls `load_circuit()` which reads the file, parses it, and validates it.

3. **Resolve inputs** (lines 876-899):
   - Checks for unknown inputs (unless `--allow-extra`)
   - Checks for missing inputs (unless `--default` provided)
   - For each input signal, parses the provided value string via `parse_input_value()`, which calls `parse_literal()` and checks that the literal width matches the signal width
   - For missing inputs with `--default`, fills with all-0 or all-1

4. **Evaluate** (lines 901-904): Calls `Evaluator(circuit).evaluate(inputs)`.

5. **Format output** (lines 906-929):
   - Sorts outputs alphabetically by name
   - In JSON mode: outputs full JSON with input/output metadata and formatted values
   - In text mode: prints `name=value` lines, one per output

### 15. parse_input_value (lines 815-823) -- How --set values are parsed

```python
def parse_input_value(value_str: str, expected_width: int, signal_name: str, filename: str = None) -> int:
    try:
        value, width = parse_literal(value_str, filename)
    except CircError:
        raise EvalError("InputValueParseError", f"Invalid input value for '{signal_name}': {value_str}")
    if width != expected_width:
        raise EvalError("InputWidthMismatchError",
                       f"Input width mismatch for '{signal_name}': expected width {expected_width}, got width {width}")
    return value
```

The value string is parsed with `parse_literal()` (same parser used for circuit file literals). The parsed width must exactly match the signal's expected width. Any `CircError` from parsing is caught and re-raised as an `EvalError`.

def main():

    if '--help' in args:     print(usage) and return 0
    if '--version' in args:  print(VERSION) or JSON and return 0
    json_mode = '--json' in args
    args = [a for a in args if a != '--json']
    command, *command_args = args
    if command == 'check':   return check_command(command_args, json_mode)
    if command == 'eval':    return eval_command(command_args, json_mode)

   286→            elif '=' in stripped:
   287→                in_declarations = False
   288→                self._parse_assignment(stripped, circuit, line_num, raw_line)
   289→            else:
   290→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid syntax: {stripped}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   291→        return circuit
   292→
   293→    def _is_declaration(self, line: str) -> bool:
   294→        parts = line.split()
   295→        return '=' not in line and bool(parts) and parts[0].upper() in ('INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE')
   296→
   297→    def _parse_declaration(self, line: str, circuit: 'Circuit', line_num: int):
   298→        parts = line.split()
   299→        if not parts:
   300→            return
   301→        keyword = parts[0].upper()
   302→        target_map = {'INPUT': circuit.inputs, 'OUTPUT': circuit.outputs, 'WIRE': circuit.wires}
   303→        if keyword not in target_map:
   304→            return
   305→        target = target_map[keyword]
   306→        existing_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   307→        for part in parts[1:]:
   308→            vec_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\[(\d+):(\d+)\]$', part)
   309→            if vec_match:
   310→                name, msb, lsb = vec_match.group(1), int(vec_match.group(2)), int(vec_match.group(3))
   311→                if msb < lsb:
   312→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: msb ({msb}) must be >= lsb ({lsb})",
   313→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   314→                if lsb < 0:
   315→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid vector range: lsb ({lsb}) must be >= 0",
   316→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   317→                if name in existing_names:
   318→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {name}",
   319→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(name) + 1)
   320→                target.append(Signal(name, msb, lsb))
   321→                existing_names.add(name)
   322→            else:
   323→                if not self._is_valid_identifier(part):
   324→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid identifier: {part}",
   325→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   326→                if part in existing_names:
   327→                    raise CircError("DuplicateNameError", f"Duplicate name: {part}",
   328→                                   self.filename, line_num, line.find(part) + 1)
   329→                target.append(Signal(part, 0, 0))
   330→                existing_names.add(part)
   331→
   332→    @staticmethod
   333→    def _is_valid_identifier(name: str) -> bool:
   334→        return bool(name) and not name[0].isdigit() and all(c.isalnum() or c == '_' for c in name)
   335→
   336→    def _parse_assignment(self, line: str, circuit: 'Circuit', line_num: int, raw_line: str):
   337→        eq_pos = line.find('=')
   338→        lhs = line[:eq_pos].strip()
   339→        rhs = line[eq_pos + 1:].strip()
   340→        if not lhs:
   341→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Missing left-hand side in assignment", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   342→        if not re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)$', lhs):
   343→            raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid left-hand side: {lhs}", self.filename, line_num, 1)
   344→        rhs_expr, _ = self._parse_expression(rhs, line_num, eq_pos + 2)
   345→        circuit.assignments.append(Assignment(lhs, rhs_expr, line_num, raw_line.find(lhs) + 1))
   346→
   347→    def _parse_expression(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   348→        text = text.strip()
   349→        if not text:
   350→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   351→        if text.startswith('{'):
   352→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   353→        expr, consumed = self._parse_primary_expr(text, line_num, start_col)
   354→        remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   355→        while remaining.startswith('['):
   356→            bracket_end = remaining.find(']')
   357→            if bracket_end == -1:
   358→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched bracket in index/slice", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   359→            bracket_content = remaining[1:bracket_end]
   360→            if ':' in bracket_content:
   361→                parts = bracket_content.split(':')
   362→                if len(parts) != 2:
   363→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   364→                try:
   365→                    hi, lo = int(parts[0].strip()), int(parts[1].strip())
   366→                except ValueError:
   367→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice indices: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   368→                expr = Slice(expr, hi, lo)
   369→            else:
   370→                try:
   371→                    idx = int(bracket_content.strip())
   372→                except ValueError:
   373→                    raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid index: {bracket_content}", self.filename, line_num, start_col + consumed)
   374→                expr = Index(expr, idx)
   375→            consumed += bracket_end + 1
   376→            remaining = text[consumed:].strip()
   377→        return expr, consumed
   378→
   379→    def _try_match_literal(self, text: str):
   380→        m = re.match(r"^(\d+'[bhd])", text, re.IGNORECASE)
   381→        if m:
   382→            prefix = m.group(1).lower()
   383→            rest = text[len(m.group(1)):]
   384→            if prefix.endswith('b'):
   385→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([01_]+)', rest)
   386→            elif prefix.endswith('h'):
   387→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   388→            else:
   389→                digits_m = re.match(r'^([0-9_]+)', rest)
   390→            if digits_m:
   391→                full = m.group(1) + digits_m.group(1)
   392→                end_pos = len(full)
   393→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   394→                    return full
   395→            return None
   396→        if text.lower().startswith('0b') and len(text) > 2:
   397→            m = re.match(r'^(0b[01_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   398→            if m:
   399→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   400→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   401→                    return m.group(1)
   402→            return None
   403→        if text.lower().startswith('0x') and len(text) > 2:
   404→            m = re.match(r'^(0x[0-9a-fA-F_]+)', text, re.IGNORECASE)
   405→            if m:
   406→                end_pos = len(m.group(1))
   407→                if end_pos >= len(text) or not text[end_pos].isalnum() and text[end_pos] != '_':
   408→                    return m.group(1)
   409→            return None
   410→        if text and text[0] in ('0', '1'):
   411→            if len(text) == 1 or not text[1].isalnum() and text[1] != '_':
   412→                return text[0]
   413→        return None
   414→
   415→    def _parse_primary_expr(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[object, int]:
   416→        text = text.strip()
   417→        if not text:
   418→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   419→        if text.startswith('{'):
   420→            return self._parse_concatenation(text, line_num, start_col)
   421→        if text.startswith('('):
   422→            depth, i = 1, 1
   423→            while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   424→                if text[i] == '(':
   425→                    depth += 1
   426→                elif text[i] == ')':
   427→                    depth -= 1
   428→                i += 1
   429→            if depth != 0:
   430→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   431→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(text[1:i-1], line_num, start_col + 1)
   432→            return expr, i
   433→        lit_text = self._try_match_literal(text)
   434→        if lit_text is not None:
   435→            value, width = parse_literal(lit_text, self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   436→            return Literal(value, width), len(lit_text)
   437→        call_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)\s*\(', text)
   438→        if call_match:
   439→            op_name = call_match.group(1).upper()
   440→            op_start = call_match.end()
   441→            paren_count, i = 1, op_start
   442→            while i < len(text) and paren_count > 0:
   443→                if text[i] == '(':
   444→                    paren_count += 1
   445→                elif text[i] == ')':
   446→                    paren_count -= 1
   447→                i += 1
   448→            if paren_count != 0:
   449→                raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched parenthesis in expression", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   450→            args = self._parse_arguments(text[op_start:i-1], line_num, start_col + op_start)
   451→            return Call(op_name, args), i
   452→        id_match = re.match(r'^([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)', text)
   453→        if id_match:
   454→            name = id_match.group(1)
   455→            return Identifier(name), len(name)
   456→        raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid expression: {text[:50]}", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   457→
   458→    def _parse_concatenation(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> Tuple[Concat, int]:
   459→        if not text.startswith('{'):
   460→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Expected '{' for concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   461→        depth, i = 1, 1
   462→        while i < len(text) and depth > 0:
   463→            if text[i] == '{':
   464→                depth += 1
   465→            elif text[i] == '}':
   466→                depth -= 1
   467→            i += 1
   468→        if depth != 0:
   469→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Unmatched brace in concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   470→        inner = text[1:i-1]
   471→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(inner)
   472→        if not raw_parts:
   473→            raise CircError("CircParseError", "Empty concatenation", self.filename, line_num, start_col)
   474→        parts = [self._parse_expression(p, line_num, start_col + 1 + idx)[0] for idx, p in enumerate(raw_parts)]
   475→        return Concat(parts), i
   476→
   477→    def _parse_arguments(self, text: str, line_num: int, start_col: int) -> list:
   478→        text = text.strip()
   479→        if not text:
   480→            return []
   481→        raw_parts = _split_comma_separated(text)
   482→        args = []
   483→        offset = 0
   484→        for raw in raw_parts:
   485→            idx = text.index(raw, offset)
   486→            expr, _ = self._parse_expression(raw, line_num, start_col + idx)
   487→            args.append(expr)
   488→            offset = idx + len(raw)
   489→        return args
   490→

   492→@dataclass
   493→class Assignment:
   494→    lhs: str
   495→    rhs: object
   496→    line: int
   497→    col: int

   499→
   500→@dataclass
   501→class Circuit:
   502→    inputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   503→    outputs: list = field(default_factory=list)
   504→    wires: list = field(default_factory=list)
   505→    assignments: list = field(default_factory=list)
   506→
   507→    def get_all_names(self) -> set:
   508→        return {sig.name for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires}
   509→
   510→    def get_signal(self, name: str):
   511→        for sig in self.inputs + self.outputs + self.wires:
   512→            if sig.name == name:
   513→                return sig
   514→        return None

   517→class Validator:
   518→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit, filename: str):
   519→        self.circuit = circuit
   520→        self.filename = filename
   521→        self.signal_types = {sig.name: 'input' for sig in circuit.inputs}
   522→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'output' for sig in circuit.outputs})
   523→        self.signal_types.update({sig.name: 'wire' for sig in circuit.wires})
   524→        self.signal_info = {sig.name: sig for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
   525→        self._all_names = circuit.get_all_names()
   526→        self._signal_widths = {name: sig.width for name, sig in self.signal_info.items()}
   527→
   528→    def validate(self):
   529→        self._check_assignments()
   530→        self._check_cycles()
   531→
   532→    def _check_assignments(self):
   533→        assigned = set()
   534→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   535→            if asn.lhs not in self._all_names:
   536→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   537→            if self.signal_types.get(asn.lhs) == 'input':
   538→                raise CircError("InputAssignmentError", f"Cannot assign to input: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   539→            if asn.lhs in assigned:
   540→                raise CircError("MultipleAssignmentError", f"Signal assigned multiple times: {asn.lhs}", self.filename, asn.line, asn.col)
   541→            assigned.add(asn.lhs)
   542→            self._validate_expr(asn.rhs, asn.line)
   543→        for sig in self.circuit.outputs + self.circuit.wires:
   544→            if sig.name not in assigned:
   545→                kind = "Output" if self.signal_types.get(sig.name) == 'output' else "Wire"
   546→                raise CircError("UnassignedSignalError", f"{kind} not assigned: {sig.name}", self.filename)
   547→
   548→    def _validate_expr(self, expr, line_num: int) -> int:
   549→        """Validate expression and return its width."""
   550→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   551→            return expr.width
   552→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   553→            if expr.name not in self._all_names:
   554→                raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}", self.filename, line_num)
   555→            return self._signal_widths[expr.name]
   556→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
   557→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   558→            self._check_index_bounds(expr, line_num)
   559→            return 1
   560→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   561→            self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   562→            if expr.hi < expr.lo:
   563→                raise CircError("CircParseError", f"Invalid slice: hi ({expr.hi}) < lo ({expr.lo})",
   564→                               self.filename, line_num)
   565→            self._check_slice_bounds(expr, line_num)
   566→            return expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   567→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   568→            return sum(self._validate_expr(p, line_num) for p in expr.parts)
   569→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   570→            self._check_operator_arity(expr, line_num)
   571→            return self._check_operator_width(expr, line_num)
   572→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   573→
   574→    def _check_index_bounds(self, expr: Index, line_num: int):
   575→        sig = self.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
   576→        if sig:
   577→            if expr.index < sig.lsb or expr.index > sig.msb:
   578→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   579→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
   580→                               self.filename, line_num)
   581→        else:
   582→            base_width = self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   583→            if expr.index < 0 or expr.index >= base_width:
   584→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   585→                               f"Index {expr.index} out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
   586→                               self.filename, line_num)
   587→
   588→    def _check_slice_bounds(self, expr: Slice, line_num: int):
   589→        sig = self.signal_info.get(expr.expr.name) if isinstance(expr.expr, Identifier) else None
   590→        if sig:
   591→            if expr.lo < sig.lsb or expr.hi > sig.msb:
   592→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   593→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for signal {sig.name}[{sig.msb}:{sig.lsb}]",
   594→                               self.filename, line_num)
   595→        else:
   596→            base_width = self._validate_expr(expr.expr, line_num)
   597→            if expr.lo < 0 or expr.hi >= base_width:
   598→                raise CircError("IndexOutOfBoundsError",
   599→                               f"Slice [{expr.hi}:{expr.lo}] out of bounds for expression of width {base_width}",
   600→                               self.filename, line_num)
   601→
   602→    def _check_operator_arity(self, call: Call, line_num: int):
   603→        op = call.operator
   604→        num_args = len(call.args)
   605→        if op not in OPERATOR_ARITY:
   606→            raise CircError("UndefinedNameError", f"Unknown operator: {op}", self.filename, line_num)
   607→        min_args, max_args = OPERATOR_ARITY[op]
   608→        if num_args < min_args or (max_args and num_args > max_args):
   609→            if max_args == min_args:
   610→                req = f"exactly {min_args}"
   611→            elif max_args is None:
   612→                req = f"at least {min_args}"
   613→            else:
   614→                req = f"between {min_args} and {max_args}"
   615→            plural = "s" if min_args > 1 or (max_args and max_args > 1) else ""
   616→            raise CircError("ArityError", f"Operator {op} requires {req} argument{plural}, got {num_args}", self.filename, line_num)
   617→
   618→    def _check_operator_width(self, call: Call, line_num: int) -> int:
   619→        op = call.operator
   620→        arg_widths = [self._validate_expr(arg, line_num) for arg in call.args]
   621→        if op in _UNARY_PROPAGATE:
   622→            return arg_widths[0]
   623→        if op in _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
   624→            for i, w in enumerate(arg_widths[1:], 1):
   625→                if w != arg_widths[0]:
   626→                    raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   627→                                   f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand {i} has width {w}",
   628→                                   self.filename, line_num)
   629→            return arg_widths[0]
   630→        if op in _SELECT:
   631→            if arg_widths[0] != 1:
   632→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   633→                               f"Selector in {op} must have width 1, got width {arg_widths[0]}", self.filename, line_num)
   634→            if arg_widths[1] != arg_widths[2]:
   635→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   636→                               f"Width mismatch in {op}: operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}, operand 2 has width {arg_widths[2]}",
   637→                               self.filename, line_num)
   638→            return arg_widths[1]
   639→        if op in _REDUCE:
   640→            return 1
   641→        if op == 'EQ':
   642→            if arg_widths[0] != arg_widths[1]:
   643→                raise CircError("WidthMismatchError",
   644→                               f"Width mismatch in EQ: operand 0 has width {arg_widths[0]}, operand 1 has width {arg_widths[1]}",
   645→                               self.filename, line_num)
   646→            return 1
   647→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown operator: {op}")
   648→
   649→    def _check_cycles(self):
   650→        dependencies = {sig.name: set() for sig in self.circuit.inputs}
   651→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   652→            dependencies[asn.lhs] = _collect_dependencies(asn.rhs)
   653→        WHITE, GRAY, BLACK = 0, 1, 2
   654→        color = {name: WHITE for name in self._all_names}
   655→
   656→        def dfs(node: str, path: list):
   657→            color[node] = GRAY
   658→            path.append(node)
   659→            for dep in dependencies.get(node, set()):
   660→                if color[dep] == GRAY:
   661→                    cycle_start = path.index(dep)
   662→                    return path[cycle_start:] + [dep]
   663→                if color[dep] == WHITE:
   664→                    result = dfs(dep, path)
   665→                    if result:
   666→                        return result
   667→            path.pop()
   668→            color[node] = BLACK
   669→            return None
   670→
   671→        for node in self._all_names:
   672→            if color[node] == WHITE:
   673→                cycle = dfs(node, [])
   674→                if cycle:
   675→                    raise CircError("CycleError", f"Cycle detected: {' -> '.join(cycle)}", self.filename)
   676→
   677→
   678→class Evaluator:
   679→    def __init__(self, circuit: Circuit):
   680→        self.circuit = circuit
   681→        self.values = {}
   682→        self._signal_widths = {sig.name: sig.width for sig in circuit.inputs + circuit.outputs + circuit.wires}
   683→
   684→    def evaluate(self, inputs: dict) -> dict:
   685→        self.values = dict(inputs)
   686→        for asn in self.circuit.assignments:
   687→            self.values[asn.lhs] = self._eval_expr(asn.rhs)
   688→        return {sig.name: self.values[sig.name] for sig in self.circuit.outputs}
   689→
   690→    def _eval_expr(self, expr) -> int:
   691→        if isinstance(expr, Literal):
   692→            return expr.value
   693→        if isinstance(expr, Identifier):
   694→            return self.values[expr.name]
   695→        if isinstance(expr, Index):
   696→            return (self._eval_expr(expr.expr) >> expr.index) & 1
   697→        if isinstance(expr, Slice):
   698→            width = expr.hi - expr.lo + 1
   699→            return (self._eval_expr(expr.expr) >> expr.lo) & ((1 << width) - 1)
   700→        if isinstance(expr, Concat):
   701→            result = 0
   702→            for part in expr.parts:
   703→                width = _expr_width(part, self._signal_widths)
   704→                result = (result << width) | (self._eval_expr(part) & ((1 << width) - 1))
   705→            return result
   706→        if isinstance(expr, Call):
   707→            args = [self._eval_expr(arg) for arg in expr.args]
   708→            return self._apply_operator(expr.operator, args, expr)
   709→        raise RuntimeError(f"Unknown expression type: {type(expr)}")
   710→
   711→    def _apply_operator(self, op: str, args: list, call: Call) -> int:
   712→        if op == 'NOT':
   713→            width = _expr_width(call.args[0], self._signal_widths)
   714→            return (~args[0]) & ((1 << width) - 1)
   715→        if op == 'BUF':
   716→            return args[0]
   717→        if op in _BINARY_PROPAGATE:
   718→            width = _expr_width(call.args[0], self._signal_widths)
   719→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
   720→            result = args[0]
   721→            for a in args[1:]:
   722→                if op in ('AND', 'NAND'):
   723→                    result &= a
   724→                elif op in ('OR', 'NOR'):
   725→                    result |= a
   726→                else:
   727→                    result ^= a
   728→            if op in ('NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
   729→                result = (~result) & mask
   730→            return result
   731→        if op in _SELECT:
   732→            return args[1] if args[0] else args[2]
   733→        if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
   734→            width = _expr_width(call.args[0], self._signal_widths)
